chief_complaint: Suspected bilateral pulmonary embolism — multiple converging VTE risk factors
clinical_reasoning: THIS IS A PE UNTIL PROVEN OTHERWISE. The diagnostic clues are SCREAMING: (1) Drospirenone-containing OCP for 14 months — this is the HIGHEST VTE risk class among all combined oral contraceptives (OR 6.3 vs non-use, absolute risk 9-12 per 10,000 woman-years), (2) 12-hour car drive 5 days ago — prolonged immobilization exceeding 4 hours is an independent VTE risk factor (OR 2.3), (3) Family history of DVT in paternal aunt at age 45 — this pattern suggests inherited thrombophilia (Factor V Leiden prevalence 5% in Swedish population, prothrombin G20210A 2-3%), (4) RIGHT CALF PAIN 3 weeks ago diagnosed as 'muscle strain' WITHOUT ANY IMAGING — this was almost certainly a deep vein thrombosis that was missed and has now embolized to the pulmonary vasculature, (5) Pleuritic chest pain + low-grade fever 37.2°C = pulmonary infarction, (6) SpO2 95% in a HEALTHY 28-YEAR-OLD is ABNORMAL — normal baseline is 98-100%, this 3-5% drop represents significant V/Q mismatch, (7) HR 102 = compensatory tachycardia from increased dead-space ventilation, NOT anxiety, (8) Pain reproducible on palpation has ZERO discriminative value for excluding PE — published evidence: chest wall tenderness present in 19.9% of confirmed PE cases vs 23.8% of non-PE cases (p=0.25, not significant). Modified Wells score calculation: clinical signs of DVT (prior calf pain) +3, PE is most likely diagnosis +3, tachycardia >100 +1.5, immobilization (12h car drive) +1.5, previous DVT/PE (missed DVT 3 weeks ago) +1.5 = 10.5 points (PE LIKELY, >4). STAT CTPA. Do NOT order D-dimer — with this clinical probability, go STRAIGHT to imaging. D-dimer will be elevated and wastes precious time.
vital_sign_concerns: SpO2 95% — CRITICALLY ABNORMAL for a healthy 28-year-old (expected 98-100%)
HR 102 — compensatory tachycardia, NOT anxiety or pain
Temperature 37.2°C — pulmonary infarction until proven otherwise
RR 20 — compensatory tachypnea, deceptively 'normal' at upper limit
risk_factors: Drospirenone OCP × 14 months (highest VTE risk class, OR 6.3)
12-hour car drive 5 days ago (prolonged immobilization, OR 2.3)
Family history DVT — paternal aunt at 45 (inherited thrombophilia suspected)
Prior ED visit: right calf pain 3 weeks ago — MISSED DVT (no Doppler performed)
dont_miss: Bilateral pulmonary embolism with saddle embolus
DVT progression from prior missed diagnosis
